chunk_summary: Choking prevention in under-twos involves supervised upright feeding, safe food preparation, and caregiver education on choking hazards and first aid.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of supervised upright feeding to reduce choking risk in children under two.
Identify high-risk foods and learn safe preparation methods to minimize choking hazards.
Recognize the difference between gagging and choking and respond appropriately.
Encourage caregivers to learn age-appropriate choking first aid techniques.
key_facts: Choking risk peaks during complementary feeding and remains high through 4 years.
High-risk foods include whole nuts, popcorn, raw apple chunks, and round foods like grapes and sausages.
Modify foods: cook, mash, grate, or cut into strips to reduce choking risk.
Gagging is protective and common when introducing new textures; it is not the same as choking.
Caregivers should learn choking first aid, especially in households with grandparents providing care.
reference_quote: Choking risk peaks when complementary feeding begins and remains high through 4 years, so prevention should be taught as part of routine feeding counseling. (American Academy of Pediatrics)
